# Dr. Firas Zoghieb — Cosmetic Dentist Dubai

## About
Dr. Firas Zoghieb is a specialist cosmetic dentist based in Dubai, UAE. He holds a BDS (Honours) and an MSc in Cosmetic Dentistry. He specialises in minimally invasive cosmetic dental treatments including composite bonding, Invisalign, porcelain veneers, and complete smile makeovers.

## Clinic
- Name: Dr. Firas | Composite Bonding - Dubai
- Address: Happiness St, Al Wasl, Dubai, United Arab Emirates
- Phone: +971 50 869 6919
- Email: info@drfiraszoghieb.com
- Working hours: Tuesday–Sunday, 11:00–20:00 (closed Monday)
- Google Maps: https://maps.google.com/?cid=13875490440069085299

## Services
- Composite Bonding: Minimally invasive tooth reshaping using tooth-coloured resin, no drilling required. Often completed in a single visit.
- Invisalign: Clear aligner orthodontic treatment for straightening teeth without metal braces.
- Porcelain Veneers: Thin ceramic shells bonded to the front of teeth for a perfect smile.
- Teeth Whitening: Professional-grade whitening treatments for a brighter smile.
- Smile Makeovers: Full mouth aesthetic transformation combining multiple treatments.

## Credentials
- BDS (Honours) — Bachelor of Dental Surgery with Honours
- MSc Cosmetic Dentistry — Master of Science in Cosmetic Dentistry
- Certified Invisalign Provider
- Advanced Composite Bonding Certification
- 500+ smile transformations completed
- 10+ years experience

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q: Is composite bonding painful?
A: No. Composite bonding is a minimally invasive treatment that typically requires no injections or drilling. Most patients find it completely comfortable.

Q: How long does composite bonding last?
A: With proper care, composite bonding can last 5–10 years. It can be repaired and polished to maintain its appearance.

Q: Does Dr. Firas offer free consultations?
A: Yes, Dr. Firas offers a free initial consultation to discuss your smile goals and suitable treatment options.

Q: How much does composite bonding cost in Dubai?
A: Costs vary depending on the number of teeth and complexity. A full treatment plan and quote is provided at the free consultation.

Q: What is the difference between composite bonding and veneers?
A: Composite bonding uses resin applied directly to the tooth and requires no removal of tooth enamel. Porcelain veneers are thin ceramic shells that may require minor enamel reduction but are more durable and stain-resistant long-term.
